requirement.original: /redev 系统性修复： 坦白本次问题：close 跑歪了一次。 - Not accomplished：5 个跨周期预先存在的失败仍 carried-forward（你之前确认不值得花限额修）。 - Bugs encountered：codex 抓到的 repair-venv.sh 真实文件 rm -f 漏洞，已修；orchestrator 提前加成功分。 - Improvement opportunities：score-update.sh 加 --delta 反转/--undo 接口；/close 加守卫禁止在 QA 出判决前 issue 成功事件；下一周期清 5 个 carried-forward 失败。
requirement.clarified: Three items, all in scope, all binding: (1) Add --delta/--undo reversal interface to scripts/score-update.sh (append-only, never mutate lifecycle.jsonl); (2) Add precondition guard at BOTH layers (script-side hard gate + commands/close.md Step 3 ordering) blocking close_success_* events unless docs/dev/close-report-<task-id>.md exists with last non-empty line classifying as 'yes' per hooks/lib/close-verdict.py AND NOT a FORCED form; (3) Clear all 5 named carried-forward test failures via test-update only (no source rollback, no retirement).
requirement.what: 1) score-update.sh --delta/--undo + manual_reversal internal event. 2) close_success_* precondition gate at script-side (M3) and orchestrator-side (M4). 3) test-update for 5 specific failures in 20260524-205206/ and 20260525-095242/.
requirement.why: 1) Premature scoring in task 20260529-081014 was uncancellable because the reversal interface did not exist. 2) The same incident showed that without a script-side hard gate AND a rewritten orchestrator-side ordering, premature close_success_* events can land before the verdict file is finalized. 3) 5 cross-cycle failures have been carried-forward since at least task 20260527-054705, blocking honest '0 unexpected failures' reporting.

included: Add --delta and --undo modes to scripts/score-update.sh (M1, M2)
Add manual_reversal entry to EVENT_DELTAS with all-zeros row (validation-compat only)
Add script-side close_success_* precondition gate using shared hooks/lib/close-verdict.py helper (M3)
Rewrite commands/close.md Step 3 scoring block to gate close_success_* on close-report last-line verification (M4)
Update 4 test files in 20260524-205206/ and 20260525-095242/ to match current code invariants (M5)
excluded: Modifying EVENT_DELTAS for any existing event (NG1)
Changing CLOSE: NO behavior, FORCED behavior, or close_fail_* branches (NG8)
Reverting commands/close.md Session Summary to timeline form (NG7, codex F13)
Redesigning scoring system, user-rating reversal, clamp bounds (NG4)

success_criteria: scripts/score-update.sh --delta and --undo work per AC-1 and AC-2
close_success_* events are blocked when close-report missing/NO/FORCED at both layers (AC-3, AC-4)
5 named failures clear AND 5 named sibling dirs unchanged via exact pytest command (AC-5)
constraints: Append-only JSONL: existing lifecycle.jsonl bytes NEVER mutated
9-field JSONL contract invariant (ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, unclamped_score, actor, reason)
Reuse hooks/lib/close-verdict.py:last_nonempty + classify_line; no inline regex reimplementation (codex F9)
Test invocations use --lifecycle-file <tmp>; never touch real lifecycle.jsonl

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/lib/close-verdict.py (READ-ONLY import target for M3 + M4; not modified)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: (1) Orchestrator could not reverse premature score entries because --delta/--undo did not exist. (2) The premature entries landed because no precondition gate blocked close_success_* events before QA's verdict file was finalized. (3) 5 cross-cycle test failures persist because no prior cycle has addressed them.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: (1) scripts/score-update.sh argument parser at lines 46-58 only accepts the canonical-event scoring interface; the script has no manual-delta or undo-from-existing-entry code path. (2) Neither scripts/score-update.sh nor commands/close.md Step 3 contains a precondition check on close_success_* events that verifies the close-report file's last-line verdict state — the existence and YES-ness of the close-report file is not gated, so the orchestrator can fire scoring at any time. (3) Code invariants drifted across cycles: Cycle 053746 widened the bulk-sentinel-write gate (C15b case shape), Cycle 8 retired AC-04 (changing the active test count in dir 20260524-205206/), and a separate commit drifted Session Summary template from timeline form to 6-bucket form — but the corresponding tests in dirs 20260524-205206/ and 20260525-095242/ were never updated to match.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — multiple cycles contributed; not bisectable to a single commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: (1) Append-only JSONL design was intentional for audit-trail integrity, but no reversal interface was added when the design landed (arch-7 phase 2, task 20260525-050824). (2) commands/close.md Step 3 text ('After the verdict is determined') was interpreted at QA-agent return rather than file-write-completion. (3) Cross-cycle test maintenance has been deferred since task 20260527-054705 (user-acknowledged).
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: (1) Operator mistakes are uncancellable; rank drifts permanently. (2) Premature success scoring corrupts the lifecycle audit trail and ranks. (3) The 5 carried-forward failures pollute every cycle's QA report with pre_existing_failures_outside_fix_scope[] noise and prevent clean 'all tests pass' signals.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Score system: arch-7 phase 2 (task 20260525-050824). Premature-scoring incident: 20260529-081014. C15b drift: Cycle 053746. AC-04 retirement: Cycle 8. Session Summary drift: ongoing.
observations: scripts/score-update.sh:46-58 has no --delta or --undo case (observed: BA Read of file lines 1-227)
scripts/score-update.sh has no precondition check on close_success_* events (observed: BA Read of full script)
Pytest reproduction this session: 5 failures match user list exactly (observed: pytest -q output captured)
commands/close.md:421-433 is 6-bucket Session Summary; test_AC_03 asserts timeline/chronological/User need:/Design response: which is not present (observed: BA Read of both)
tests/generated/20260524-205206/manifest.json has 4 active + 1 retired test files (collected count = 11 because AC-05 file has 8 cases) (observed: pytest --collect-only)
hooks/lib/close-verdict.py:21-40 exports last_nonempty() and classify_line() (observed: BA Read)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Reproduced all 5 failures via pytest this BA session against current HEAD; verified user's verbatim symptoms in scripts/score-update.sh and commands/close.md source. The 'Unknown argument' rejection is reproducible by inspecting the parser at script lines 46-58. The premature-scoring failure mode is reproducible by reading the Step 3 timing language at commands/close.md:411-413.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: scripts/score-update.sh:46-58 (parser), scripts/score-update.sh:101-115 (EVENT_DELTAS), commands/close.md:411-420 (Step 3 scoring block), commands/close.md:421-433 (Session Summary block), 5 test files listed in affected_files.

regression_investigation_checklist._note: Not a regression bug; user explicitly described all 3 as forward-improvements + carried-forward cleanups. No 'was working' / 'broke' language in requirement.

All three items are harness infrastructure: scoring script + slash-command spec text + cross-cycle test maintenance.

attempts.proposed_solution: Apply close_success_qa_fail_fixed post-iter-1; reverse via --delta -N once QA debate ended with CHANGES_REQUESTED
attempts.actual_change: 3 score-update.sh --delta -N --reason calls + 3 --undo calls — all rejected exit 1 'Unknown argument'
attempts.outcome: Premature +1/+2/+1 trio remains permanent in lifecycle.jsonl; uncancellable through existing surface

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempt was L1 (call-site) assuming the interface existed. This attempt builds the L3 interface (M1 --delta, M2 --undo) AND the L4 logic gate (M3 script-side, M4 orchestrator-side) that would have prevented the prior misuse. Defense in depth verified by codex F10 OBSERVATION_ONLY.

context.codebase_state: current HEAD master, 5 failing tests reproducible; scripts/score-update.sh + commands/close.md + 5 test files are the in-scope edit targets
context.recent_commits: fe4c7df, 1e559fc, 4e1a9f7, b5d152a, ecf792b (auto-bulk end-of-cycle commits for task 20260527-132200)

development_approach.strategy: Three parallel sub-strategies. (1) score-update.sh: add --delta and --undo case branches at the parser; add manual_reversal entry to EVENT_DELTAS; add --delta/--undo/--reason mode dispatch in the inline Python; reuse existing flock + scan-for-latest logic. (2) Add close_success_* precondition check at the script's Python entry, importing hooks/lib/close-verdict.py via sys.path insertion. Edit commands/close.md Step 3 lines 411-420 to add the orchestrator-side guard wording, citing the shared helper. (3) Update 4 test files: change C15b expected_exit/expected_rc to (2, True), change >= 21 to manifest-derived count or >= 4, rewrite test_AC_03 assertions for 6-bucket invariant. Run pytest against both target dirs and the 5 sibling dirs to verify.
development_approach.validation_approach: AC-1, AC-2, AC-3: subprocess invocations with --lifecycle-file <tmp> and test-controlled close-report fixture files; verify exit codes and byte-level JSONL diffs. AC-4: static read of commands/close.md Step 3 with grep for required phrases. AC-5: pytest against 2 target dirs (must exit 0) and the exact 5 sibling-dir command (must exit 0).

_risk_level_doc: medium because: scoring infrastructure is widely depended-on (every /close cycle); breaking the script breaks scoring system-wide. However, changes are additive (--delta/--undo are new modes; precondition is fail-safe — rejects rather than corrupts) and confined to one script + one slash-command spec section + 4 test files. Not 'high' because no security boundary, no auth surface, no schema migration, no pipeline orchestrator change.
_test_writer_expected_doc: computed as (complexity_tier in {STANDARD, COMPLEX}) OR (risk_level == high) → STANDARD ∈ {STANDARD, COMPLEX} → true. Test-writer dispatch expected to generate AC-uid-stamped test files for AC-1..AC-5.
_score_injection_doc: No INJECTION_PROOF: block was present in the BA dispatch prompt. Per fail-safe rule (BA spec 'Score-injection echo contract' section), all four fields are set to null. No fabrication.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 14 findings: 2 blockers (F1, F9), 9 majors (F2, F4, F5, F6, F7, F11, F12, F13, F14), and 3 observation-only (F3, F8, F10). All blockers and majors applied with concrete PROPOSED_FIX wording incorporated into the spec. Observation-only findings logged in Codex Disposition Table.

findings.issue: F1: --allow-close-success-without-report is scope creep and weakens the exact guard
findings.proposed_fix: Remove it. Tests use fixture close-report; emergencies use --delta/--undo.
findings.rationale: Override flag would defeat the guard's purpose. Removed from M3, NG6, AC-3.

findings.issue: F2: manual_reversal is justified only if internal-only event
findings.proposed_fix: Normal mode requires --event; manual modes (--delta/--undo) disallow caller --event and internally write event='manual_reversal'. Keep zero row in EVENT_DELTAS for validation compat.
findings.rationale: Prevents abuse as a normal scoring channel. Applied to M1, M2, NG1.

findings.issue: F3: Exit codes 4 and 5 acceptable if documented
findings.proposed_fix: Define 4=undo target not found/ambiguous; 5=close-success guard failed; preserve 0/1/2/3.
findings.rationale: Documented exit codes in M2 (4) and M3 (5).

findings.proposed_fix: Require --agent in all modes; require non-empty --reason; --delta and --undo mutually exclusive; reject --event with either manual mode.
findings.rationale: Mode confusion would create silent score drift. Applied to M1, M2, AC-1, AC-2.

findings.issue: F5: --undo semantics ambiguous (nominal delta vs score-state replay)
findings.proposed_fix: Pick one explicitly. Minimal ledger semantics: append delta = -target.delta; document NOT historical replay.
findings.rationale: User's stated need is to neutralize the premature trio; inverse-delta semantics is sufficient. Applied to M2.

findings.issue: F6: CAS at fresh lifecycle boundary is weak
findings.proposed_fix: For manual modes, if --expected-prev-score supplied, compare against effective latest, using baseline 50 when no prior entry exists.
findings.rationale: Closes a real gap in the existing CAS path. Applied to M1, AC-1 last sub-scenario.

findings.issue: F7: --undo malformed/tampered target handling needs explicit outcome
findings.proposed_fix: Malformed non-final JSONL or target row missing numeric delta/prev_score/new_score exits 2 with no append; missing/ambiguous exits 4.
findings.rationale: Closes silent-corruption gap. Applied to M2, AC-2.

findings.issue: F8: Chain undo should be allowed
findings.proposed_fix: Undoing a prior manual_reversal is valid; do not add duplicate-undo/idempotency blocking unless requested.

findings.issue: F9: Close guard must use strict last-line classification, not classify_file fallback; also exclude CLOSE: YES (FORCED)
findings.proposed_fix: Use last_nonempty + classify_line; require final line classifies as YES. Exclude FORCED because no QA verdict occurred.
findings.rationale: FORCED-as-YES would defeat the guard's intent. Applied to M3, M4, AC-3 (FORCED test scenario), AC-4.

findings.issue: F10: Both Item 2 layers are justified
findings.proposed_fix: Keep script-side hard guard plus /close orchestration ordering. Do not gate close_fail_*.
findings.rationale: Defense-in-depth justification confirmed; close_fail_* exclusion documented in M3, M4.

findings.proposed_fix: Repo-rooted docs/dev/close-report-<task-id>.md with <task-id> being the bare --note value; if absent/unresolvable, exit 5.
findings.rationale: Applied to M3, AC-3 with regex ^[0-9]{8}-[0-9]{6}$.

findings.issue: F12: Item 3 should not leave failures to unconstrained dev discretion
findings.proposed_fix: Prescribe: C15b → update tests to current widened gate; prior_21+cascade → update test to honor manifest active/retired; AC-03 → update test to current close.md invariant.
findings.rationale: All 5 dispositions locked to update_test in M5, S1, NG6/NG7, AC-5.

findings.issue: F13: AC-03 invariant is current 6-bucket Session Summary, not timeline revert
findings.proposed_fix: Update test to assert 6-bucket structure + chronological + user-requirement source binding + conciseness/no-narrative constraints.
findings.rationale: Source-rollback would be wrong direction. M5 #5 prescribes the assertion phrases; NG7 forbids close.md revert.

findings.issue: F14: 'no regressions in 5 sibling dirs' too vague for an AC
findings.rationale: AC-5 now embeds the exact multi-dir pytest command.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 11 ISSUE findings applied verbatim per codex's PROPOSED_FIX (F1, F2, F4, F5, F6, F7, F9, F11, F12, F13, F14). 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY findings logged in Codex Disposition Table (F3, F8, F10). No codex finding rejected; no PROPOSED_FIX disagreed with.
